name: Ascension to Morron de Sierra Espuna - circular from Alhama de Murcia
name_original: Ascensión al Morrón de Sierra Espuña — circular desde Alhama de Murcia
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alhama De Murcia are primarily defined by the diverse terrain of the Sierra Espuña Natural Park, which features rolling hills, steep mountain passes, and lush green forests. This region offers varied landscapes, from challenging mountain ranges to scenic routes alongside the Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras and the unique geological formations of the Barrancos de Gebas. The area provides a network of well-maintained roads suitable for road cycling, with significant elevation gains in its higher sections. Best road cycling routes around Alhama De Murcia The most popular road cycling route is Ascension to Morron de Sierra Espuna - circular from Alhama de Murcia, a 42.0 miles (67.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountain terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal loop from Moriana, a difficult 32.6 miles (52.5 km) path. This route offers views of the Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal and varied landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Plaza de la Balsa Vieja – Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal loop from Alhama de Murcia, a 25.2 miles (40.6 km) trail leading through the Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal area, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Road cycling around Alhama De Murcia is defined by mountain ranges, green forests, and badlands, offering a range of experiences from challenging climbs to more moderate rides. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult ascents. The routes in Alhama De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from nearly 20 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alhama De Murcia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alhama De Murcia?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Alhama De Murcia documented on komoot, offering a wide range of experiences. These routes are primarily found within the diverse terrain of the Sierra Espuña Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alhama De Murcia?
A: The region enjoys a mild and generally dry Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for cycling for a significant part of the year. The best times are typically between &lt;strong&gt;February and June&lt;/strong&gt;, and again from &lt;strong&gt;September to November&lt;/strong&gt;, when temperatures are most pleasant.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or more relaxed rides?
A: Yes, Alhama De Murcia offers several easier routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10322078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Balsa Vieja – Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal loop from Alhama de Murcia&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 25.2-mile (40.6 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 42 minutes to complete, offering a more moderate experience through the Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal area.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: The Sierra Espuña Natural Park is renowned for its challenging climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008600385/ascension-to-morron-de-sierra-espuna-circular-from-alhama-de-murcia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascension to Morron de Sierra Espuna - circular from Alhama de Murcia&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 42.0-mile (67.6 km) route with substantial elevation gain, taking you through mountain terrain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal loop from Moriana&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 32.6-mile (52.5 km) path with significant climbing.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in the region?
A: Road cycling routes often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You can experience the unique geological formations of the Barrancos de Gebas, or enjoy scenic views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de Algeciras Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra Espuña Natural Park itself offers lush green forests and breathtaking mountain ranges.

Q: Are there any notable mountain passes for road cyclists in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to famous climbs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt;, a Category 1 climb topping out at almost 1,200m, known as a training ground for professional cyclists. La Espuña is also the highest road cycling climb in the Murcia region, offering fantastic views.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008600385/ascension-to-morron-de-sierra-espuna-circular-from-alhama-de-murcia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascension to Morron de Sierra Espuna - circular from Alhama de Murcia&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example of a challenging circular route. The region also features routes like the Espuroad, which offers several circular options.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Alhama De Murcia?
A: The routes in Alhama De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from nearly 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain passes to peaceful, relatively car-free roads, and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra Espuña Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal?
A: Yes, several routes provide views of the Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal loop from Moriana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10322078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Balsa Vieja – Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal loop from Alhama de Murcia&lt;/a&gt; are two popular options that feature this scenic waterway.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical sites or local attractions?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, some routes may pass near local points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40376623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alhama de Murcia – Bar La Plaza loop from Alhama de Murcia&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride that starts near the historic Castle of Alhama de Murcia, allowing for a blend of cycling and cultural exploration.

Q: What kind of road surfaces can I expect on these routes?
A: The roads in the Sierra Espuña area are generally well-maintained, providing smooth surfaces ideal for road cycling. Even some gravel roads in the wider region are wide and in excellent condition, though the routes listed here are specifically for road bikes.
tips: Next to the junction of the 515 highway, we find these views that will accompany us during a good part of the route along the C-25 with impressive views.
In the middle of the climb to Collado Bermejo from the La Perdiz recreational area, there is a succession of about 18 zigzag curves that is a classic to climb with the road bike.

Located in the Sierra Espuña and at an altitude of 1,201 m, we reach the Bermejo pass accompanied by a pine forest during the ascent and where we find a beautiful viewpoint. At this point, a brief false flat is imposed to recover energy and breath and contemplate the panorama and places as beautiful as Umbría de Peña Apartada, Peña Apartada itself, Cerro de la Garita, and of course, the deep and green Valley of the Espuna River. To the north, we find the Morrón de Espuña, and to the west the Barranco de Enmedio, El Purgatorio and the Pedro López hill.

At this point we arrive at Collado Mangueta, surely with our legs well loaded from kilometers of ascent. Here we have other impressive views of the other side of the Sierra. At this point we can decide whether to go up through the restricted area with no access to the top at the same Military Base, where they will surely recommend us to leave the place or turn around.
